content.problemStatement:
Chef is observing stock prices.

You are given an array pricespricesprices where prices[i]prices[i]prices[i] is the price of a given stock on the ithi^{\text{th}}ith day.

Chef wants to maximize his profit by choosing **one day to buy** and a **different future day to sell**.

Return the **maximum profit** Chef can achieve. If no profit is possible, return 000.

**Function Declaration****Function Name**findMaxProfitfindMaxProfitfindMaxProfit – Computes the maximum achievable profit by buying on one day and selling on a later day.

**Parameters**
pricespricesprices : A list/array of integers where 

prices[i]prices[i]prices[i] = stock price on day iii.
**Return Value**
Returns an **integer** — 

the maximum profit Chef can make.
If no profitable transaction is possible, return 000.
Constraints:
2≤n≤1052 \leq n \leq 10^52≤n≤105
0≤prices[i]≤1040 \leq prices[i] \leq 10^40≤prices[i]≤104

Input Format

nnn → number of days
Next line → **n integers** representing stock prices


Output Format
Print the maximum profit Chef can achieve.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer n (the number of days). The second line contains n space‑separated integers, the stock prices for each day.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the maximum profit Chef can achieve by buying on one day and selling on a later day. If no profit is possible, output 0.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Buy on day 3 (price = 1) and sell on day 4 (price = 7). Profit = 7 - 1 = 6.
Prices keep falling, so no profit can be made.
Buy on day 1 (price = 1) and sell on day 5 (price = 5). Profit = 5 - 1 = 4.

solution.approach: Traverse the price array while keeping track of the smallest price seen so far (minPrice). For each price, compute the profit if we sold on that day: price - minPrice. Update the answer with the maximum profit encountered. This works because the best sell day for any future price must be paired with the minimum price that occurred earlier. The algorithm runs in linear time and uses constant extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case, where n is the number of days. Overall O(T * n).
